channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: How Japan Lost 3 Million People in Five Years
item.description: Japan’s population fell by more than 3 million over the past five years, a record drop that underscores the depths of the country’s accelerating demographic crisis.
item.dc:creator: Javier C. Hernández, Pablo Robles and Kiuko Notoya

item.title: Shrey Parikh Wins Scripps National Spelling Bee After Tense Spell-Off
item.description: Shrey Parikh, a 14-year-old from California, claimed the 2026 Scripps National Spelling Bee title on Thursday in a dramatic spell-off that tested his speed and precision.
item.dc:creator: Julie Yoon

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times

item.title: Los hombres jóvenes que pueden influir en las elecciones intermedias de EE. UU.
item.description: Muchos hombres de la generación Z que votaron por Donald Trump están consternados por su mandato. Pero dicen que tampoco escuchan un discurso atractivo de los demócratas.
item.dc:creator: Kellen Browning and Emma Goldberg

media:content.media:credit: Anna Watts for The New York Times

item.title: Blue Origin Rocket Explodes on Florida Launchpad
item.description: A rocket built by the Jeff Bezos-owned space company, Blue Origin, blew up during a test at the Cape Canaveral Space Force Station in Florida.
item.dc:creator: Nailah Morgan

media:content.media:credit: Malcolm Denemark/USA Today, via Imagn Images, via Reuters
media:content.media:description: The Blue Origin launchpad seen in flames from Cocoa Beach, Fla. on Thursday night.

item.title: These Parents Wanted Their Children’s Park Back. They Finally Got It.
item.description: George Washington Bridge Park, also known as Dolphin Park, had been closed for nearly a decade, but residents in Manhattan pushed relentlessly for its reopening.
item.dc:creator: Anusha Bayya
item.category: Port Authority of New York and New Jersey

media:content.media:credit: Madison Swart for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Monica Sanchez and her son, Andy, played at Dolphin Park in Washington Heights on Thursday. The park had been closed for several years.

item.title: The Knicks’ Playoff Run Has Brought a Peculiar Harmony to New York
item.description: The team’s playoff run has brought a curious sense of amity to the city.
item.dc:creator: Matt Flegenheimer

media:content.media:credit: José A. Alvarado Jr. for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A blazing playoff run has electrified New York Knicks fans of all ages, in a city unaccustomed to such things from its basketball team.

item.title: Natural Light. Soaring Ceilings. At Penn Station? That’s the Plan.
item.description: A week after Amtrak selected a developer to remake the dreary rail hub, details of its rebirth are emerging. The cost is less clear.
item.dc:creator: Patrick McGeehan
item.category: Moynihan Train Hall at Pennsylvania Station (Manhattan, NY)

media:content.media:description: An overhaul of Pennsylvania Station in New York could cost $7 billion.

item.title: New York Wants to Restrict ICE Enforcement. ICE Has Other Ideas.
item.description: New York leaders changed state immigration laws to hold federal agents accountable for their deportation tactics, but their efforts will face opposition from the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Grace Ashford and Hamed Aleaziz

media:content.media:description: New legislation adopted by New York State lawmakers bars federal immigration agents from wearing masks, as they did last year at a staging area in Lower Manhattan.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for May 29, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Russian Drone Hits Romanian Apartment Building, Officials Say
item.description: NATO condemned “Russia’s recklessness” and confirmed that it was in touch with the Romanian authorities.
item.dc:creator: Cassandra Vinograd

media:content.media:description: The site where a drone hit a building in Galati, Romania, on Friday, close to the border with Ukraine.

item.title: Jimmy Kimmel Finds Glee in President Trump’s Approval Rating
item.description: A new poll shows it sitting at 34 percent. “He has the same approval rating as ‘Paul Blart: Mall Cop,’” Kimmel said on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix

item.title: Europa se acerca a una guerra comercial con China. Estas son las razones
item.description: A medida que Pekín adopta políticas comerciales más agresivas, los dirigentes y las empresas europeas se inquietan por su dependencia de los productos chinos.
item.dc:creator: Jeanna Smialek and Alexandra Stevenson

media:content.media:description: Vehículos eléctricos de fabricación china a la espera de ser exportados en el puerto de Hangzhou, al este de China, en mayo.

item.title: Hooters se reinventa como restaurante familiar
item.description: La cadena conocida por sus pequeños uniformes y sus “noches en bikini” intenta cambiar su imagen. ¿Alguien quiere un pañalero de Hooters?
item.dc:creator: Alessandra Schade and Sydney Krantz

media:content.media:description: Una reunión reciente de un equipo infantil de béisbol en el Hooters de East Brunswick, Nueva Jersey.

item.title: Palabra del día en inglés: ‘diatribe’
item.description: Esta palabra ha aparecido en 31 artículos en NYTimes.com en el último año. ¿Puedes usarla en una frase?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Word of the Day: diatribe
item.description: This word has appeared in 31 articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Hungary Showed How to Defeat an Autocrat
item.description: Hungary did it. The United States can, too.
item.dc:creator: M. Gessen and Máté Bartha

media:content.media:credit: Máté Bartha for The New York Times

item.title: 8 Are Rescued From Texas Roller Coaster Stuck Almost 100 Feet Off the Ground
item.description: The riders waited for about four hours, facing up, on the roller coaster at the Pleasure Pier park in Galveston. There were no injuries, the authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Francesca Regalado

item.title: The World’s 50 Best Restaurants Announces Its 2026 North America List
item.description: At a ceremony in New Orleans on Thursday, a Midwestern fine-dining standout took the top spot.
item.dc:creator: Julia Moskin

item.title: Europe Is Edging Closer to a Trade War With China. Here’s Why.
item.description: As cheap goods pour in, threatening the continent’s manufacturing sector, a search for solutions is becoming increasingly urgent.
item.dc:creator: Jeanna Smialek and Alexandra Stevenson

media:content.media:description: Chinese-made electric vehicles awaiting export at the port in Hangzhou, eastern China, in May.

item.title: She Said Yes. Then a Crowd at Madison Square Garden Cheered.
item.description: Owen Brown met Sophie Everhard 11 years ago at an event where she was working the door. He proposed before a Billie Eilish concert last fall.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Block

item.title: Corrections: May 29, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Friday, May 29, 2026.

item.title: He Wanted to Kiss a Million Times. Could We Do It?
item.description: My optimistic, romantic husband never would have guessed what would happen to us.
item.dc:creator: Denise DiIanni

item.title: She Dreamed of Getting Married at Grandma’s ‘Pink Castle’
item.description: Korren Campbell wed Jake Degler along the Miles River at her ‘Oma’s’ Alhambra-style pink house, in Easton, Md.
item.dc:creator: Rosalie R. Radomsky

item.title: From Investor to Royal, Then Came a Wedding Fit for a Queen
item.description: When Arian Reed, a founder of an Atlanta-based venture-capital firm built by and for women of color, became a queen in Africa’s Ivory Coast, Benjamin Mougarbel, a Ghanaian businessman, took notice.
item.dc:creator: Tammy LaGorce

item.title: Finding Someone to ‘Conquer the World’ With
item.description: Elsa Alvarado and Hunter Stoll instantly clicked over politics and a shared love of dogs. Since then, they have fostered four together.
item.dc:creator: Shivani Vora

item.title: Taylor and Travis (Different Ones) Step Into the Spotlight
item.description: Taylor Hayes and Travis Wickboldt of Wisconsin became the face of Zola’s “Wedding of the Year” campaign, appearing on a billboard in Times Square.
item.dc:creator: Shivani Vora

item.title: Quote of the Day: A.I. Now Gives Scams a Slick Disguise. Here’s How to Tear It Off.
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Friday, May TK, 2026.

item.title: Scripps Spelling Bee 2026 Takeaways: Shrey Parikh Wins in Spell-Off
item.description: The competition came down to a spell-off. Contestants were eliminated early, and a sixth grader became a crowd favorite.
item.dc:creator: Maria Cramer

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Shrey Parikh, a 14-year-old from Rancho Cucamonga, Calif., broke the record for the most words spelled correctly in a spell-off.

item.title: Protesters Arrested at Immigration Detention Center as Tensions Rise
item.description: For nearly a week, relatives of migrants at Delaney Hall have said that some detainees have been on a hunger strike at the New Jersey facility.
item.dc:creator: Ana Ley and Mark Bonamo

media:content.media:description: Federal officials arrested demonstrators late Wednesday outside the Delaney Hall immigration detention center in Newark.

item.title: Should We Let Robots Kill People?
item.description: Are we ready for autonomous drones to make life-and-death decisions on the battlefield? Christian Brose, the chief strategy officer of Anduril Industries, explains on “Interesting Times” that the Pentagon’s official policy leaves the door for autonomous weapons wide open.
item.dc:creator: Interesting Times
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: Is the U.S. Prepared for a Major War?
item.description: America has fired “something like eight years’ worth of Tomahawk missile production” in Iran, Christian Brose, the chief strategy officer of Anduril Industries, says on “Interesting Times,” where he and the Opinion columnist Ross Douthat discuss the limitations of America’s arsenal of “luxury” weapons.
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: After New Push by the Bolsonaros, U.S. Labels Brazilian Gangs as Terrorist Groups
item.description: Following through on threats, the Trump administration has designated Brazil’s two largest drug gangs as terrorist groups.
item.dc:creator: Ana Ionova

media:content.media:description: Members of the Red Command being arrested last year in Rio de Janeiro. The U.S. designated the gang a terrorist group on Thursday.

item.title: Families Settle Suit Over Deaths in Baltimore Bridge Collapse
item.description: The owner and the operator of the cargo ship that crashed into the Key Bridge settled cases brought by several victims’ families, but other claims are headed to trial.
item.dc:creator: Campbell Robertson

media:content.media:credit: Pete Kiehart for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The families of five of the six men killed in the Key Bridge collapse two years ago announced a settlement with the cargo ship’s owner and operator.

item.title: Blue Origin Rocket Blows Up on Florida Launchpad During Test
item.description: The rocket, built by the Jeff Bezos-owned space company, was to carry 48 satellites into space. Blue Origin reported on social media that “all personnel have been accounted for.”
item.dc:creator: Kenneth Chang

media:content.media:credit: Malcolm Denemark/USA Today, via Imagn Images, via Reuters
media:content.media:description: The Blue Origin launchpad seen in flames from Cocoa Beach, Fla. on Thursday night.

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for May 29, 2026
item.description: Hemant Mehta opens our solving weekend.
item.dc:creator: Sean McGowan

media:content.media:credit: Cornell Watson for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Don’t lose your footing on this slick Friday puzzle.

item.title: 3 Dead After Large Fire at Dallas Apartment Building
item.description: A damaged gas line led to a five-alarm fire in the Oak Cliff neighborhood. At least four were injured, and firefighters believe they may find more victims.
item.dc:creator: Jesus Jiménez

item.title: ‘Hacks’ Leaves Them Laughing
item.description: In an interview, Jean Smart and Hannah Einbinder discuss the series finale, their offscreen relationship and why comedy is just like sex.
item.dc:creator: Alexis Soloski and Chantal Anderson

media:content.media:credit: Chantal Anderson for The New York Times

item.title: In Carroll Lawsuits Inquiry, Scrutiny Turns Toward Private Citizens Who Antagonized Trump
item.description: The Justice Department is said to be examining the funding of lawsuits brought by E. Jean Carroll, an author who has never sought a public role, political power or governmental authority.
item.dc:creator: Glenn Thrush and Benjamin Weiser

media:content.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: E. Jean Carroll, the author and columnist, leaving court in Manhattan in 2024 after a hearing on one of her lawsuits against Donald J. Trump.

item.title: J.F.K. Airport to Begin Screening Some Passengers for Ebola
item.description: The U.S. has begun rerouting travelers from the Democratic Republic of Congo, South Sudan and Uganda to select airports for enhanced health screenings, to identify those who may have been exposed to the Ebola virus.
item.dc:creator: Karoun Demirjian

media:content.media:description: Travelers pass through John F. Kennedy International Airport, which will begin screening passengers from Ebola-stricken areas.

item.title: After Traffic Stop for Phone in Right Hand, She Called Out the Problem
item.description: The deputy insisted he saw a driver with a phone in her right hand. That turned out to be impossible, and the case was dropped.
item.dc:creator: Mark Walker

item.title: Death Toll Rises in Washington Paper Mill Explosion
item.description: At least eight are now confirmed dead, and firefighters believe there are three more bodies they have yet to reach, making the crisis the worst industrial disaster in the state’s modern history.
item.dc:creator: Anna Griffin and Aaron West

media:content.media:description: Community members placed electric candles at a vigil at R.A. Long Park after a fatal chemical storage tank failure in Longview, Wash.

item.title: Italian Police Uncover Dead Mob Boss’s $230 Million Business Empire
item.description: When Matteo Messina Denaro died in 2023, many of his secrets died with him. That began to change with a tip about a wealthy Sicilian woman’s significant assets in a tiny European principality.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni

media:content.media:description: Matteo Messina Denaro being led from a police station after his arrest in Palermo in 2023.

item.title: Curating in the Cross Hairs: Is This the Smithsonian Chief’s Last Show?
item.description: Lonnie G. Bunch III, the Smithsonian secretary under pressure from the White House, organized an exhibit exploring America’s founding ideals.
item.dc:creator: Robin Pogrebin

media:content.media:credit: Caroline Gutman for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: “I love thinking creatively about, how do you protect the Smithsonian?” said Lonnie G. Bunch III, the institution’s secretary. “How do you make sure issues of scholarship and integrity are there?”

item.title: Suit Says Black Infants Were Subjected to Experimental Vaccine Without Consent
item.description: The babies were part of a vaccine trial for a respiratory virus in the 1960s and died shortly after. Their families said they had been unaware of the trial until recently.
item.dc:creator: Remy Tumin

media:content.media:description: Benjamin Crump is representing the families of two Black infants who were unknowingly enrolled as test subjects in a mid-1960s vaccine trial for R.S.V.

item.title: Trump’s Iran Approach Sows Confusion
item.description: President Trump’s pendulum swings on Iran have often seemed driven by mood and moment rather than any discernible strategy.
item.dc:creator: Michael Crowley and Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: President Trump’s shifts reflect a political tug of war between hawkish supporters urging him to hit Iran harder and noninterventionists urging him to make a quick deal.

item.title: Alaska Authorities Race to Rescue 4 Climbers Who Fell on Mount McKinley
item.description: The condition of the climbers was not immediately known.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra E. Petri

media:content.media:description: Weather conditions on the mountain were improving enough to allow for helicopter operations, officials said.

item.title: Who Are the 9 Scripps Spelling Bee Finalists?
item.description: The remaining spellers are 12 to 14, and represent five states as well as the District of Columbia.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Ahn

item.title: With Pitchford v. Cain, The Supreme Court Protected Jury Rights. Now It Must Go Further.
item.description: All Americans benefit from the outcome of this case. And yet the decision highlights just how much work still needs to be done.
item.dc:creator: Avital Fried

media:content.media:credit: Aleksey Kondratyev for The New York Times

item.title: Does a Taco Truck Belong on ‘The 100 Best Restaurants in N.Y.C.’ List?
item.description: Wesley Morris and The Times’s restaurant critic Ligaya Mishan discuss why a fine-dining establishment could be ranked alongside a taco truck on the “100 Best Restaurants” list.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Rocklin, Austin Mitchell and Felice Leon
item.category: The 100 Best Restaurants in New York City 2026

item.title: How ‘New Yorkiness’ Shaped List of the Best N.Y.C. Restaurants
item.description: Wesley Morris and Ligaya Mishan, The Times’s co-chief restaurant critic, discuss what makes Mama Lee, one the 2026 best restaurants in New York City, idiosyncratic and “scrappy.”
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Rocklin, Austin Mitchell and Felice Leon
item.category: A&A Bake & Doubles Shop (Brooklyn, NY, Restaurant)
